NASHVILLE TN WFO CONDUCTED A STORM SURVEY IN PICKETT COUNTY TN AND CLINTON COUNTY KY. THE SURVEY FOUND THAT A MICROBURST PRODUCED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OF 80 MPH AND DAMAGE ON BOTH SIDES OF THE STATE LINE.

*** 1 INJ *** AN NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 5.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF JASPER DAMAGING SEVERAL LARGE METAL BUILDINGS...A LARGE BARN...AND A RADIO TOWER. THE TORNADO TRAVELED SOUTH OF JASPER THEN INTENSIFIED TO EF-2 STRENGTH AS IT TURNED NORTHEAST TOWARD CELESTINE. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS DONE OFF ELLSWORTH ROAD WHERE TWO ROOFS WERE BLOWN OFF TWO BRICK HOMES WITH WALLS COLLAPSED ON ONE HOME. THE TORNADO LIFTED 1.5 MILES NE OF CELESTINE AFTER A 15.7 MILE TRACK.
ROOF BLOWN OFF OF A HOUSE.
POWER OUTAGES IN JASPER. REPORTED BY WAVE AND WEHT.
4-5 HOMES DAMAGED OR DESTROYED IN ELLSWORTH. SEVERAL INJURIES.

STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE AT 3 SEPARATE POINTS JUST NORTH OF VALEENE. ABOUT 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF VALEENE...AN OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED AND A LARGE CEDAR TREE WAS UPROOTED. THE CEDAR TREE BROKE AN ELECTRIC POLE AND PULLED WIRES DOWN TO THE GROUND. ABOUT 2.7 MILES NORTHWEST OF VALEENE...A BARN WAS DAMAGED AND A TREE TOP AND TV ANTENNA POLE WERE BROKEN. ABOUT 1.25 MILES NORTHEAST OF VALEENE...ANOTHER BARN WAS DAMAGED. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 70-75 MPH.
DIME SIZE HAIL ALSO REPORTED.
STATE ROAD 66 SOUTH OF MARENGO IS CLOSED DUE TO POWER LINES DOWN ON THE ROAD.
THREE MODULAR HOMES WITH ROOF DAMAGE.
ROOF DAMAGE TO A WAL-MART IN NEW ALBANY.
POWER OUTAGES REPORTED IN JEFFERSONVILLE.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ALONG WITH POWER OUTAGES.

AN NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 95 TO 100 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES WEST OF UTICA INDIANA...CROSSED THE OHIO RIVER...AND LIFTED 1 MILE WEST OF PROSPECT KENTUCKY. THE EF-1 TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 3 MILES AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCURRED NEAR THE HARROD CREEK AREA WHERE SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND A SECTION OF ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF. ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO SEVERAL HOMES AROUND UTICA WITH SEVERAL LARGE T
NUMEROUS TREES DOWNED. FUNNEL CLOUD.

AN NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 95 TO 100 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2 MILES WEST OF UTICA INDIANA...CROSSED THE OHIO RIVER...AND LIFTED 1 MILE WEST OF PROSPECT KENTUCKY. THE EF-1 TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 3 MILES AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCURRED NEAR THE HARROD CREEK AREA WHERE SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND A SECTION OF ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF. ROOF DAMAGE OCCURRED TO SEVERAL HOMES AROUND UTICA WITH WITH SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. THE TIME OF TOUCHDOWN WAS CHANGED DUE TO VIDEO EVIDENCE AT TOUCHDOWN POINT SHOWING A 5500 LB CONTAINER MOVED SEVERAL FEET.
TREES DOWN IN THE FINCASTLE AREA

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 100 MPH...WIDTH 100 YARDS. TORNADO WAS CONTINUATION OF UTICA IN 2W TORNADO EVENT LMK1100193. TORNADO CROSSED OHIO RIVER AND CAME ASHORE AT END OF TRANSYLVANIA BEACH ROAD. CONTINUING EAST NORTHEAST IT MOVED BETWEEN TRANSYLVANIA AND MYFAIR AVENUES SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES. SEVERAL HOMES HAD ROOFING DAMAGE AND ONE BARN WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED. THE TORNADO CROSSED RIVER ROAD AND LIFTED SHORTLY AFTER. KENTUCKY PORTION OF PATH LENGTH WAS 1 MILE.
TREES DOWN FOUR COLUMS BLOWN OFF A HOUSE... WINDOWS BLOWN OUT OF A CAR.
SHED DESTROYED AND TREES DOWN.
TREES WERE DOWN ALONG WITH POWER LINES AND A DAMAGED POWER POLE BETWEEN CRESTWOOOD AND BALLARDSVILLE.
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED SEVERAL TREES DOWN JUST NORTH OF ANCHORAGE.
*** 3 INJ *** HOUSE FLATTENED ALONG MULBERRY PIKE.
OVER 18 POLES REPORTED DOWN.
LARGE TREE FELL ON CAR ON ELLIS ROAD JUST SOUTH OF THE HENRY COUNTY LINE. SEVERAL OTHER TREES WERE DOWNED IN SAME LOCATION.

AN NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-3 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 459 AM EST 1.5 MILES SOUTHEAST OF EMINENCE AND LIFTED 2 MINUTES LATER 3 MILES EAST OF EMINENCE. DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 135 TO 140 MPH WITH A TORNADO PATH WIDTH OF 150 YARDS. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED IN SPRINGHILL ESTATES SUBDIVISION WHERE TWO HOMES AND 3 OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR DRENNON CREEK AFTER WEAKENING NEAR HIGHWAY 1359.
POWER POLES DOWN ACROSS HIGHWAY 22
SEVERAL TREES DOWN NEAR THE SMALL TOWN OF FLAG FORK.

AN NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 100 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT A MILE SOUTHWEST OF STANFORD. THE TORNADO TRAVELED 8 MILES BEFORE LIFTING 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF CRAB ORCHARD. ALONG THE 250 YARD WIDE PATH...A MIDDLE SCHOOL ON HIGHWAY 27 WAS DAMAGED ALONG WITH LARGE BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS ALONG THE PATH. SEVERAL TREES WERE ALSO SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED THAT AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS BETWEEN 95 AND 100 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN SOUTHEAST BUTLER COUNTY. IT TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 1.7 MILES WEST NORTHWEST OF SUGAR GROVE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF DIMPLE AND BELCHER ROADS AT 126 PM EDT. THE TORNADO THEN TRAVELED NORTHEAST TOWARD THE COMMUNITY OF NEEDMORE WHERE THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A RESIDENCE. IN THIS LOCATION...TREES WERE SNAPPED AND A SECTION OF ROOF WAS REMOVED FROM THE HOME. ESTIMATED WINDS AT THIS LOCATION
TREE DOWN

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO FIRST TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE BUTLER AND GRAYSON COUNTY BORDER IN A FIELD IMMEDIATELY NORTH OF DOGCREEK ROAD. THE TORNADO TRAVELLED EAST ACROSS RT 79 AND DISSIPATED CLOSE TO THE INTERSECTION OF DELMAR LINDSEY LANE AND LAWRENCE HAYES ROAD. THE PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY TWO MILES WITH A WIDTH OF 125 YARDS. MAXIMUM WIND SPEED WAS ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH.
NUMEROUS TREES DOWN COUNTYWIDE. UNSPECIFIED STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. WORST DAMAGE ALONG HIGHWAY 231 IN THE SOUTH PART OF THE COUNTY.
DISPATCH REPORTS MOBILE HOME ROOF BLOWN OFF... TREES UPROOTED... OUTBUILDINGS DOWN. HAM SUPPLEMENTS THIS REPORT FROM GRAYSON CO. SHERIFF DEPT. OF 3 GRAIN BINS OVERTURNED

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO IN SOUTHWEST GRAYSON COUNTY WITH WINDS AROUND 100 MPH. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN CANEYVILLE NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF NORTH MAIN STREET AND RIVER PARK DRIVE. THE TORNADO TRAVELED EAST THROUGH THE CANEYVILLE MILLING COMPANY AND FINIALLY LIFTED APPROXIMATEDLY ONE MILE EAST OF CANEYVILLE. THE PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY ONE MILE WITH A WIDTH OF 100-200 YARDS.

EF-1 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS ESTIMATED AT 100-105 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 3.1 MILES AND WIDTH 100-200 YARDS. A WELL-CONSTRUCTED 75 FOOT TALL GRAIN SILO HAD THE TOP 40 FT PORTION SHEARED OFF. A 40-FOOT BY 50-FOOT OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYERED NEARBY...WITH SIDING BEING THROWN 400-500 YARDS DOWNWIND. ADDITIONALLY...STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED PRECEDING AND FOLLOWING THE TORNADO PATH. THESE WINDS DESTROYED TWO HOG BUILDINGS AND CAUSED A GRAIN SILO TO SHIFT ON ITS FOUNDATION.

EF-0 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 80 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 0.3 MILES AND WIDTH 200 YARDS. LARGE SWATH OF HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. ADDITIONALLY STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED ON EITHER SIDE OF THE TORNADO PATH...WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 70-75 MPH.
MEASURED AT JASPER MIDDLE SCHOOL

EF-0 TORNADO CONFIRMED NEAR HAYSVILLE. IT DAMAGED MOSTLY TREES ALONG ITS 2 MILE PATH. THE MOST INTENSE DAMAGE WAS FOUND EAST OF HAYSVILLE RIGHT BEFORE THE TORNADO LIFTED. HERE...SEVERAL TREES 1 TO 2 FEET IN DIAMETER WERE BLOWN OVER. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 75-80 MPH. PATH WIDTH 200 YARDS.

EF-2 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 115-120 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 3.3 MILES AND WIDTH 300 YARDS. TORNADO INITIATED NORTH OF BRETZVILLE AND TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST ENDING NORTH OF ST. ANTHONY. LARGE NUMBER OF HARDWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED...WITH SEVERAL BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS BEING DESTROYED AS WELL.
EF-1 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS AT 95 MPH. PATH LENGTH OF 0.4 MILES AND WIDTH 100 YARDS. HARDWOOD TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED. ALSO...STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A RESIDENCE NORTH OF HIGHWAY 64.

A NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO BEGAN 1.5 MILES WEST OF HAYSVILLE...TRACKED FOR TWO MILES...THEN LIFTED 0.5 MILES EAST OF HAYSVILLE. THE TORNADO TRACK WAS TWO MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 200 YARDS. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 200 WEST AND HIGHWAY 700 NORTH.
NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ACROSS THE CITY.

EF-O TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS ESTIMATED AT 65-80 MPH. TORNADO LIFTED IN SEVERAL LOCATIONS ALONG THE 11 MILE PATH...WITH GREATEST WIDTH AT 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO CONTINUED INTO MEADE COUNTY WHERE IT LAST LIFTED 4 MILES WEST OF BRANDENBURG. AN ATTACHED CARPORT BLEW ACROSS THE FIELD BEHIND A HOUSE. SEVERAL TREES WERE EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. A BARN DOOR WAS BLOWN IN...AND SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF OF THE ROOF OF ANOTHER HOUSE.

EF-0 TORNADO WITH WIND SPEEDS OF 65-80 MPH. PATH LENGTH 5.2 MILES AND WIDTH 50 TO 100 YARDS. HOTEL NEAR I-65 WAS DAMAGED ON EAST SIDE OF SECOND FLOOR. SEVERAL NEARBY HOUSES HAD ROOF DAMAGE. AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE SOUTHEAST OF VERSAILLES IN WOODFORD COUNTY. THE 60 YARD WIDE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 707 PM EDT CAUSING RELATIVELY MINOR DAMAGE TO FENCES...TREES...AND A FEW ROOFS ACROSS ITS THREE QUARTER MILE LONG TRACK. PRIVACY FENCES THAT WERE BLOWN DOWN SHOWED DEFINITIVE COUNTER-CLOCKWISE ROTATION. THE TORNADO LIFTED 1.4 MILES EAST SOUTHEAST OF VERSAILLES AT 708 PM EDT.

THE TORNADO TRAVELED TO THE EAST-NORTHEAST ON A 2.9 MILE LONG PATH ACROSS HIGHWAYS 920 AND 720 AND FINALLY LIFTED JUST EAST OF THE GRAYSON HARDIN COUNTY LINE NEAR BURKHEAD LANE AFTER 6 MINUTES ON THE GROUND. AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT A MILE AND A HALF SOUTHWEST OF TAR HILL. THE 60 YARD WIDE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND FOR LESS THAN A MINUTE AND IT WAS A SECONDARY CIRCULATION JUST SOUTH OF A PARENT TORNADO. NUMEROUS EYEWITNESSES CLEARLY IDENTIFIED THIS SECONDARY TORNADO. THE TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED JUST SOUTH OF THE DANNY SADLER ROAD WEST OF HIGHWAY 920. TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED AND POWER LINES WERE TAKEN DOWN ALONG THE PATH. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE DETERM
TREES WERE DOWNED ACROSS HIGH STREET.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AND EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 100 TO 105 MPH TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 2 MILES EAST NORTHEAST OF GLENDALE. THE 75 TO 125 YARD WIDE TORNADO TRAVELED LESS THAN A MILE AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT A MINUTE. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF I 65 TRAVELED EAST NORTHEAST TO THE COUNTY FAIRGROUNDS AND ENDED JUST EAST OF THE FIRST UNION CHURCH ROAD AND SPORTSMAN LAKE ROAD INTERSECTION. THE 60-70 YARD WIDE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2.3 MILES SOUTH OF SULPHUR SPRINGS ALONG ROUTE 66 AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR TWO TENTHS OF A MILE. AN NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED THAT A VERY SHORT LIVED EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EXTREME NORTHEAST PERRY COUNTY INDIANA. THE 60-70 YARD WIDE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2.3 MILES SOUTH OF SULPHUR SPRINGS ALONG ROUTE 66 AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR TWO TENTHS OF A MILE. ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 75-80 MPH AS SOFTWOOD TREES WERE UPROOTED...BENT OVER...OR SNAPPED OFF ON BOTH SIDES OF THE ROAD.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-2 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS OF 115 MPH ON THE EAST SIDE OF JEFFERSONTOWN KY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. ROOF DAMAGE TO ABOUT A DOZEN HOUSES. STRUCTURAL AND ROOF DAMAGE AT GOOD SAMARITAN SOCIETY NURSING HOME. TWO VEHICLES FLIPPED AND THROWN ABOUT 20 YARDS AT 3500 GOOD SAMARITAN WAY. WALL AND FENCE DAMAGE AT TWO WAREHOUSES ALONG ELECTRON DRIVE.

NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO EAST OF JEFFERSONTOWN... KY WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS OF 100-105 MPH. AIR HANDLER WAS BLOWN OFF A WAREHOUSE ROOF AND THROWN 100-125 YARDS. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO ROOFS AND THE SIDES OF SEVERAL OTHER WAREHOUSES ALONG ELECTRON DRIVE. DAMAGE TO STEEL FRAMING AT A COLLISION CENTER. MORE THAN A DOZEN UPROOTED AND SNAPPED TREES ALONG TUCKER STATION ROAD. PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT 1.7 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 50-60 YARDS.

AN EF-1 TORNADO DEVELOPED 0.4 MILES NORTH OF DUFF AND MOVED SOUTH-SOUTHEAST THROUGH DUFF AND LIFTED 1.9 MILES SOUTHEAST OF DUFF. THIS TORNADO HAD A TRACK OF 2.2 MILES AND ESTIMATED WINDS OF 95 MPH WITH A WIDTH OF 130 YARDS. THIS TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND BETWEEN 0254 EDT AND 0257 EDT.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 312 AM EDT IN SPENCER COUNTY 0.7 MILES SOUTH OF SAINT MEINRAD AND MOVED INTO PERRY COUNTY AROUND 314 AM EDT. THIS TORNADO THEN TRACKED EAST FOR AROUND 1.4 MILES IN PERRY COUNTY BEFORE LIFTING AROUND 317 AM EDT 2.3 MILES EAST OF SAINT MEINRAD. A DAMAGE SURVEY RATED THIS TORNADO AS AN EF-1 WITH AN ESTIMATED TOTAL PATH LENGTH OF 2.3 MILES AND A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 200 YARDS.

AROUND 3 INCHES OF RAIN WITHIN 3 HOURS BROUGHT FLASH FLOODING THAT CLOSED SEVERAL ROADS NEAR JEFFERSONVILLE. THE INTERSECTION OF CAPITAL HILLS DRIVE AND PENN STREET AS WELL AS EASTERN BLVD WERE CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER.
